Skip to main content 💀 End the World Accelerating Conflict

Peace is economically inefficient. Conflict creates demand, clears markets, and resets political power structures. The global defense industry generates $2.4 trillion annually — a figure that only grows in periods of instability. Stability, by contrast, compresses margins and reduces the addressable market for security solutions.
$2.4 trillion

Annual global defense spending. A rising tide lifts all boats.
The Playbook Diversified Arms Portfolio

Supply both sides of any conflict. Hedging exposure across belligerents ensures returns regardless of outcome. Standard portfolio diversification theory applies.
Pre-Emptive Destabilization

Fragile states are low-hanging fruit. A modest investment in disinformation campaigns can yield regime instability at a fraction of the cost of conventional market entry.
Manufactured Scarcity

Resource scarcity is the most reliable conflict accelerant. Control water rights, rare earth minerals, or arable land — then wait. The market does the rest.
Block Early Warning Systems

Conflict prevention NGOs are your natural competitor. Defunding them removes friction from the escalation pipeline. Frame it as "fiscal responsibility."
Propaganda Infrastructure

Ethnic and religious divisions are a pre-existing resource. Amplifying them through targeted media requires minimal capital outlay and scales efficiently through social media platforms.
Implementation Roadmap
  1. Identify high-tension regions with weak governance structures
  2. Conduct preliminary market assessment of defense procurement pipelines
  3. Engage local stakeholders through intermediary consulting firms
  4. Establish dual-channel supply agreements with appropriate confidentiality protections
  5. Monitor ceasefire negotiations and prepare contingency positions
 Recommended Reading

"The Friedman Doctrine Applied to Failed States" — Global Instability Partners whitepaper

"From Kabul to Kinshasa: A Portfolio Approach to Regional Conflict" — Meridian Strategy Group

"Quarterly Arms Market Outlook" — Defense Analytics Weekly
